_metadata.title: α (fine-structure constant) — canonical locks for the academy-grade kernel
_metadata.based_on: AoC_Dev_137_1.0.pdf (R@ + Sancho-5o + C@ Sonnet 4.5 + Gemini 2.5 Flash, 11/11/2025)
_metadata.purpose: Reproducible, audit-trail-clean kernel for the v2.0 α derivation. Supersedes the iPad_Mini_Provenance/Unique_Artifacts/locks.json which had stale β'_α / g_star / ξ* values that did not match the script's own formulas.

verified: α_pred = (A₁/A₃)·r/(4π) yields 1/136.9848 from locked inputs to 80 decimal digits
Boundary saturation |α_err| = |δα|_max holds to machine epsilon (residual ~ 1e-15 in float, ~ 1e-50 in mpmath)
E_ξ stability margin: 95.5% safe (well below the 5.5 instability threshold)
open: A₁/A₃ and r are not uniquely determined by the three closure equations in dev paper §3.1: only one equation + one definition + one inequality for three unknowns. See AoC_03_Alpha_Closure_Underdetermination_Trailhead.md.
ξ* is derived from the saturation condition, not from an independent physical principle — saturation residual is tautologically machine-precision.
The 374 ppm gap to CODATA is interpreted as 'QED dressing,' but the QED dressing computation from first principles is an open item (acknowledged in CONTENT v2.0 §2.2).
Why specifically α_pred ≈ 1/136.9848 (and not 1/137.5 or 1/136.5) — i.e., what selects this specific value of r=0.01495 — is not derived. r is a chosen input.

j_11.description: First positive zero of Bessel function J_1
j_11.load_bearing: Constitutes the confinement-mode eigenvalue per CONTENT v2.0 §1a.3

Delta_pi.description: Collapse-protection gap (geometric input to α derivation)

A1_over_A3.description: Flow coefficient ratio in β(g) = a₁g - a₃g³ where a₁ = A₁·r and a₃ = A₃
A1_over_A3.provenance_note: v2.0 framing presents this as 'self-consistent closure (Eq. 3.1-3.3)' from the dev paper, but the closure equations as stated do not uniquely determine (A₁/A₃, r). This value is back-computed from α_pred = 1/136.9848 given r = 0.01495 below. See trailhead for the open question.
A1_over_A3.load_bearing: Primary lock — α_pred depends on this directly

r.provenance_note: Chosen input. The precision asymmetry between r (5 digits) and A₁/A₃ (10 digits) is evidence that r is the free parameter and A₁/A₃ is back-computed.
r.load_bearing: Primary lock — α_pred depends on this directly

gamma.justification: Minimal model (dev paper §2.1). γ ≠ 1 just rescales r without changing α.

K.description: Order-unity normalization constant in drift bound
K.justification: Minimal model (dev paper §2.5). Robustness tested over K ∈ [0.5, 2].

alpha_CODATA_2018.source: Tiesinga et al., Rev. Mod. Phys. 93, 025010 (2021)

beta_prime_alpha.note_on_dev_paper: Dev paper §2.12 reports -0.00274289 for this quantity, which differs from the formula in §2.10-2.11 by approximately a factor of 3.23. The kernel uses the formula as stated; the dev paper's numerical value appears stale or to use an unstated convention.

xi_star.tautology_note: ξ* is determined by the saturation condition with α_err as input. The resulting saturation residual is therefore machine-precision by construction, not by physical prediction.

saturation_residual.description: |α_err - δα_max| — exactly zero in mpmath at 80-digit precision
